title: The Art Fair Boat
story_html:
Leo was a shy boy who loved building things. For the school's art fair, his class had a challenge: each student had to make an original project and share it with the whole town.

Leo's main idea was to build a small wooden boat with a rope tied to the front, like a real fishing boat. His friend Mia helped him plan it. "I can't wait to see your boat," she said. "I trust you can finish it."

Every afternoon after school, Leo worked on his project. He wanted to develop his idea slowly, testing each piece to check its quality before adding it to the boat. As he sanded the wood, Leo would imagine the boat sailing on real water. Some days he grew tired, but he kept trying. He would relax for a few minutes, then go back to work.

One day, Leo needed a special piece of wood from the store across town. Mia agreed to deliver a note to her uncle at the shop, asking him to save the wood. Leo had to make a decision: walk through the busy street alone or wait for his dad. He decided to cross carefully, holding his dad's hand at the light.

At the shop, Leo's dad paid with coins from his pocket. The shop owner, an old man with a bright smile, gave Leo the wood for free. "You are a trustworthy boy," he said. "I recognize hard work when I see it."

On the day of the fair, Leo felt nervous walking into the public square. He read the schedule and found his spot. When it was time, he stood up to present his boat. People looked closely at the little mark he had carved on the side — his own name.

The judges were impressed and gave Leo a small trophy. No thief could ever take away his pride. His father smiled and said the fair would always lead him toward new dreams, and he should protect that feeling forever.

quality.def: How good or bad something is.

decision.def: A choice that you make after thinking.

present.def: To show or give something to a group of people.

trust.def: To believe that someone is honest and reliable.

imagine.def: To make a picture or idea in your mind.

deliver.def: To bring something to a place or person.

friend.def: A person you like and enjoy spending time with.

rope.def: Strong, thick string used for tying or pulling.

shy.def: Nervous or uncomfortable around other people.

cross.def: To go from one side to the other.

feel.def: To experience an emotion or sensation.

recognize.def: To know someone or something because you have seen it before.

bright.def: Full of light, or very clever.

pocket.def: A small pouch sewn into clothing for holding small things.

try.def: To make an effort to do something.

read.def: To look at written words and understand them.

develop.def: To grow or change into something more advanced.

public.def: Open for anyone to see or know.

lead.def: To guide someone, or to go toward a place.

original.def: The first, or not a copy.

challenge.def: Something difficult that tests your ability.

mark.def: A visible sign or symbol on something.

protect.def: To keep someone or something safe.

questions.qtext: In "'You are a trustworthy boy,' he said," what does trustworthy mean?
questions.explain: A — trustworthy means able to be trusted, which is why the shop owner gave Leo the wood for free.

questions.qtext: In "He wanted to develop his idea slowly, testing each piece to check its quality," what does develop mean?
options: to grow or change something into something more advanced
questions.explain: B — develop means to grow or change into something more advanced, matching how Leo built his idea step by step.

questions.qtext: In "The shop owner, an old man with a bright smile, gave Leo the wood for free," what does bright mean?
questions.explain: C — bright describes something full of light or cheerful, showing the shop owner was friendly and kind.

questions.qtext: Why did Leo decide to cross the street with his dad instead of alone?
options: He forgot where the shop was
The street was busy, so he chose to wait for his dad and cross carefully
His dad told him he could not go to the shop
Mia asked him to wait for her instead
questions.explain: B — the story says Leo had to decide between walking alone or waiting for his dad, and he chose to cross carefully with him.

questions.qtext: How did Leo feel about his boat project by the end of the story?
options: Embarrassed that people looked at it closely
Proud, after his hard work was noticed by the judges
Worried that someone would steal his trophy
Disappointed that his boat was not finished in time
questions.explain: B — the judges were impressed and Leo felt pride in his work, which his father wanted him to protect and remember.
